rationale: The cited paper is used in the citing paper's hypothesis development for its free-vs-paid boundary condition, not just as a general background mention. In the theory section, Bond et al. (2019) is specifically invoked as evidence that a free offer shifts attention to nonmonetary/process-related considerations, which supports the logic for H3. That logic is then operationalized and tested in Study 4 (free vs. paid education), so the citation has a meaningful conceptual role in the paper's model setup.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Bond et al.'s insight about how zero price changes consumer focus to justify its claim that free education offerings induce a process-oriented mindset. It then uses this applied logic to predict and test attenuation of the PDB-by-appeal effect under free (vs. paid) conditions.
